name: AgriBusiness Global Podcasts
description: The Ag Tech TALK podcast will cover how ag tech developments are affecting the crop protection market. This includes new products, trends, analysis, and other information that those in the crop protection can use to stay up to date.

recent_episodes.title: Using Connected Technology to Protect Grain Quality
recent_episodes.description: On this episode of Ag Tech Talk, we sit down with Alan Lockwood, Senior Product Manager for Grain Conditioning at GSI, to explore how connected technologies are transforming grain storage and drying. From real-time monitoring and early spoilage detection to remote dryer management during labor-tight harvest seasons, Lockwood breaks down how tools like GrainView and GSI Connect are helping farmers, retailers, and co-ops protect grain quality, reduce losses, and make smarter operational decisio

recent_episodes.title: Helping Growers Succeed With Biologicals: Certis on Training, Adoption, and Integration
recent_episodes.description: In this Sustainable Podcast episode by AgriBusiness Global, Gregory Rogers, Director of Technical Marketing and Communication at Certis Biologicals, outlines what the company does to help growers understand, adopt, and optimize biologicals within integrated crop management programs.

recent_episodes.title: Rethinking Land Value in the Age of Modern Agronomy
recent_episodes.description: In this episode of Ag Tech Talk by AgriBusiness Global (ABG), Jim Zimmer, Operating Partner at Granite Creek Capital and Co-Founder of Moraine Farmland Partners, discusses how farmland valuation is changing in the era of advanced agronomy and precision technology.

score_reason: Duplicate listing; same evaluation as previous entry on ag tech and innovation.

key_themes: AI and technology adoption in traditional agriculture
Market trends, regulatory changes, and competitive positioning
M&A, business strategy, and industry consolidation
Product development and commercialization in crop inputs
Data-driven decision making and supply chain innovation
enrichment.relevant_episode: How AI and Geospatial Tech Are Transforming Carbon Measurement and Supply Chains — Andreas bridges the gap between AI hype and practical implementation for commodity producers, directly addressing how traditional ag companies can leverage data intelligence.
enrichment.coverage_gap: The human side of AI adoption in traditional industries: overcoming organizational resistance, changing mindsets on commodity boards, and the say-do gap between what growers claim they want and what they actually buy — no guest has tackled the behavioral economics and change management of AI in crop protection.
enrichment.suggested_hook: The guy who built Canada's largest food agency and sold it now teaches AI strategy to commodity boards—here's why your crop protection customers say they want data-driven decisions but aren't making them.
